nameMeanings.name: Scott
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (Down): habitational and ethnic name from Middle English Scot ‘man from Scotland’. There is no evidence that the surname denoted either of the earlier senses of Scot as ‘(Gaelic-speaking) Irishman’ or ‘man from Alba’, the Gaelic-speaking region of Scotland north of the river Forth. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English and Scottish: from the rare Middle English personal name Scot (Old English Scott, possibly also Old Norse Skotr), only certainly attested in northern England.

variants.name: Scutt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Pierre.
English: nickname for a short person, from Middle English scut(e) ‘short’, or else for a swift runner, from Middle English scut ‘hare’.

nameMeanings.name: Marjory
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual modern spelling of Margery . It seems to have arisen as the result of folk etymological association of the name with that of the herb marjoram ( compare Rosemary ). This word is of uncertain origin; its Middle English and Old French form was majorane, without the first -r-.

en.lifeSummary: When Marjory Scott was born in 1389, in Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Sir William Scott of Balweary, was 39 and her mother, Elizabeth Harborne, was 29. She had at least 5 sons and 1 daughter with Sir John Melville - Second Lord of Raith. She died in 1424, in Fife, Scotland, at the age of 35.
